steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 claims it is obvious that UNESCO’s selection boosted awareness and tourism. Text 2 pushes back with "However": cities must create intentional marketing strategies or visitors won’t even know about the designation. The question asks what Text 2’s author would most likely say in response to that underlined claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Many people worked together…chefs deserve the most credit': Text 2 never discusses who deserves credit for the selection. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Belo Horizonte may have benefited…but tourism can likely be further increased by making more potential visitors aware': This concedes some benefit while arguing that awareness requires active promotion — exactly Text 2’s argument. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The naming was long overdue': Text 2 says nothing about the timing of the selection. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Benefits extend well beyond tourism': Text 2 focuses narrowly on tourism and marketing; it does not claim broader benefits. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
